title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read an article from which six sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–G the one which fits each gap (37–42). There is one extra sentence which you do not need to use.

content.title: The Green Arena: Inside the World of Competitive Gardening
content.text: At the Chelsea Flower Show each May, a peculiar kind of tension fills the air. Exhibitors who have spent twelve months — and sometimes considerably longer — planning and building their show gardens pace the paths with a mix of pride and anxiety. Judges move slowly from plot to plot, noting the precision of planting, the quality of construction and the coherence of the overall design. (37) [37] La competición de jardinería es un proceso exigente y minucioso. [/37] For those who reach the podium, a gold medal here is considered the most coveted prize in the horticultural world. || Chelsea is the most famous event, but competitive gardening extends far beyond London. Village flower shows, county horticultural society competitions and specialist national championships take place across Britain throughout the growing season. (38) [38] La jardinería competitiva se practica en todos los niveles. [/38] At the grassroots level, local shows remain important social occasions, attracting participants who have been competing against each other — and their neighbours — for decades. || The vegetable categories are often the most fiercely contested. Growers enter onions polished to a high sheen, leeks of perfect proportions and runner beans measured to a fraction of a millimetre. (39) [39] La presentación de los vegetales exige preparación casi científica. [/39] Soil mixtures, feeding regimes and watering schedules are treated as trade secrets, rarely shared even among longstanding competitors. || Giant vegetable growing occupies its own eccentric corner of the competitive gardening world. Every September at Harrogate, the National Giant Vegetables Championship attracts competitors who have devoted entire growing seasons to producing record-breaking pumpkins, marrows and cabbages. (40) [40] Los vegetales gigantes rompen records de peso que parecen imposibles. [/40] The current world record for the heaviest pumpkin stands at over a thousand kilograms — a figure that would have seemed impossible a generation ago. || Competitive gardening also has a therapeutic dimension that is easy to overlook. Many participants cite the meditative quality of garden work — the rhythm of digging, planting and weeding — as central to their wellbeing. (41) [41] La jardinería competitiva tiene beneficios importantes para la salud mental. [/41] Studies have linked regular gardening to reduced levels of stress hormones and improved mood, effects that the competitive gardening community seems to embody in the quiet dedication its members bring to their plots. || As interest in growing food at home has surged, a new generation of younger gardeners is beginning to enter local shows. (42) [42] Los jóvenes están renovando el mundo de la jardinería competitiva. [/42] Traditional stalwarts welcome the fresh faces with a mixture of enthusiasm and, perhaps, a quiet sharpening of competitive instincts.

paragraphs.A: Achieving the necessary size requires specialist cultivation techniques, precise nutrition and considerable good fortune with the weather.
paragraphs.B: For many longstanding participants, the stress of competition is entirely outweighed by the social bonds it creates.
paragraphs.C: The standards demanded are extraordinarily high: even slight blemishes or imperfect symmetry can cost an exhibitor points.
paragraphs.D: Their arrival is gradually changing the culture of events that have been dominated by older generations for many years.
paragraphs.E: From single-plot allotment shows to prestigious national championships, the competitive spirit in horticulture operates at every scale.
paragraphs.F: Several hospital garden programmes have found that patients who tend competitive plots recover faster than those with no gardening activity.
paragraphs.G: Even the way exhibits are arranged and labelled is scrutinised, with marks deducted for untidy presentation.

questions.explanation: C describes the high standards required of exhibitors, including imperfections that cost points, supporting the image of rigorous judges.
E generalises that competition exists at all levels, from local plots to national championships, before focusing on local events.
G adds that presentation and labelling are also strictly evaluated, completing the picture of almost scientific preparation.
A explains the specialised techniques and climatic risks needed to achieve record sizes before the world record figure.
F provides clinical evidence of the benefits of gardening in patient recovery, reinforcing the therapeutic dimension.
D describes how young people are changing the culture of events, anticipating the reaction of veteran participants.
